topic: Someone receives a friend request from a childhood bully
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification popped up on my screen, a jarring intrusion into a quiet Tuesday afternoon. It was a profile picture I hadn't seen in fifteen years—a slightly crooked smile and the same challenging smirk that used to make my stomach clench. It was Mark. My childhood tormentor. My fingers hovered over the 'Accept' button, a cold knot tightening in my chest. Every memory of his sneers, the way he’d deliberately tripped me in the schoolyard, rushed back, sharp and unwelcome. I quickly minimized the message, my breath catching in my throat. I stared at the blank wall, the silence amplifying the frantic thumping beneath my ribs. What was I supposed to do with this?

[story 2]

"Hey, it's Sarah," the message read. The casualness of the greeting felt like a physical blow. I reread the message three times, my eyes darting back and forth across the screen, searching for any hint of deceit. A wave of heat washed over my face, making my cheeks burn. I felt a distinct urge to delete the entire application, to erase this unwelcome digital tether. My hands trembled slightly as I finally tapped 'Accept.' The ensuing silence in my small apartment was deafening, broken only by the frantic drumming of my pulse against my ribs. I couldn't look away from the screen, a heavy weight settling onto my shoulders.

[story 3]

I hadn't expected the ping. When I saw the name, a sudden, unwelcome clench seized my jaw. It was him. The boy who had built his entire existence around making me feel small. A deep, uncomfortable sinking sensation spread through my core. I took a long, shaky breath, trying to steady the tremor in my hands before I even considered responding. My mind raced, cycling through old scenarios, trying to calculate the fallout of this interaction. Finally, with a sigh that seemed too loud in the quiet room, I clicked through. The ensuing awkwardness felt suffocating, like standing too close to a fire.
